observations.recommendation:

financial statements on April 28, 2025.
1.7 We commended the management, particularly the Municipal Accountant, for his
compliance with the proposed audit adjustments and submission of the revised
financial statements for CY 2024.

2.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reconcile
the account, revert the unreleased checks to Cash in Bank-LCCA account
and recognize the appropriate liability or equity accounts. We further
reiterated our recommendation to properly reflect the actual deposit in
transit and outstanding checks in the BRS as at year end.

3.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
erroneous recording of the transactions as at year end.

4.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking disbursement
vouchers and liquidation reports with complete supporting documents and
henceforth, adhere to the prescribed timeline in the submission of the paid
disbursement vouchers and documents.

5.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to remit all unremitted withholding taxes, premium
contributions, and loan repayments, and to ensure that all amounts withheld
in a given month are remitted in full and on time, within the prescribed due
dates.

6.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to review and revise the

Person to ensure that the budget allocated for GAD PPAs is fully utilized by

Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking paid
disbursement vouchers relative to the LGSF-SBDP of NTF ELCAC projects
to the Audit Team and observe the timely submission of accounts of the LGU.

Reports to support the claim of wages. Likewise, require the Disbursing
Officers and Municipal Accountant to submit the paid payrolls and its
supporting documents to the Audit Team.

prepare adjusting entry to correct the affected accounts in the financial
statements.

3.17 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal

pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS 17 and effect the necessary adjusting

4.7 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Accountant to
coordinate in order to immediately transfer the cash corresponding to the
year-end balance of the QRF and MOOE from General Fund to Trust

5.21 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (a) ascertain the cause of negative balance of the cash on
hand and reclassify the transaction; (b) refrain from recognizing the
amount of uncompleted projects as Accounts Payable; and (c) reverse the
erroneous journal entry made; and (d) prepare adjusting entries to

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the
collections from Due to NGAs to its proper account to correct the

7.11 We recommended that the Management strictly monitor the project
implementation to ensure completion of the project by setting time frames
and target dates so that constituents could avail of the benefits of the
project and improve the living and economic conditions of the
constituents.

8.7 We recommended that management comply with the provisions set forth
in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 to assure implementation of

9.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the HRMO and
the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-

11.6 We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit
copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Tean within five
days from execution and issuance thereof.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to cancel stale checks in accordance with
Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual, and submit to the
Accounting Office a Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks at the
end of the year;
b.) Instruct the Municipal Account to submit the lacking vouchers, provide
the general and subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank - LCCA and
regularly prepare and submit the Monthly BRS, ensuring that
outstanding checks should no longer include stale, unreleased and
encashed checks, and prepare adjusting entry to record the book
reconciling items, as follows:
Accounts and Explanation Debit Credit
General Fund
Cash in bank - LCCA ₱ 30,629.11
Accounts Payable ₱ 30,629.11
To restore stale and unreleased checks
to Cash in Bank

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
review and prepare the respective subsidiary ledgers, record the
depreciation expense for CY 2021 and adjust the erroneous recording of
transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to refrain from recording cash advances directly as expense and prepare
adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the recording of
transactions. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should properly record
and classify transactions to the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to properly record and classify transactions to the appropriate
accounts in the LGU books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LGU officials
concerned to submit the lacking DVs with the related supporting documents.
Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Health Officer to
submit the proof of distribution of drugs, medicines and other medical supplies
to end-users to support the recording of drugs and medicines to expense
accounts.

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer that payments
of various procurement be made by issuance of check directly to the
payee/supplier/creditor; and
b) Require the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to prepare
liquidation report per cash advance to determine any excess thereof; and
to utilize cash advance according to the purpose for which it was granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to (i) submit the supporting documents for online payments to BIR and the
adjustments made during the year; and (ii) remit in full and on time within the
prescribed due date all taxes withheld in a certain month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the
fund transfer to the barangays and monitor the implementation of the sub-
projects and submission of the liquidation report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer, Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit
the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and
cancelled checks, if any. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure that all disbursement vouchers and their supporting
documents, copies of cancelled checks, and other transaction documents are
submitted timely and regularly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to render their accounts and submit the required financial
reports within the reglementary period. Henceforth, strictly observe Section
347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of
financial accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials to
do the following courses of action:
a) The Municipal Assessor to update the Assessment Roll and furnish the
Municipal Treasurer a copy on or before December 31 of the current
year;
b) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified listing of
taxpayers and their respective tax due and furnish a copy to the
Municipal Accountant; and
c) The Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET Receivable at the
beginning of the year based on the report submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the
Municipal School Board, prepare SEF Budget before the ensuing year to
identify and implement PPAs which require fund support from the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials to
do the following courses of action:
a) Municipal Budget Officer to control the budget appropriations and
obligations properly to ensure that expenditures charged under the fund are
eligible, has an existing and not exceeding the approved appropriation; and
b) Municipal Engineer to supervise and inspect the projects to ascertain that the
amount paid to the laborers is equal to their work accomplishments, so that
the work plan from the Program of Works be followed and prevent incurring
more expenses over the approved appropriation.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAO for
QRF, MF and CO;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to present the details of LDRRM
Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format
provided under COA Circular 2012-02; and
c) Instruct the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and
Report on Utilization of DRRM using the prescribed format provided
under the same circular.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person
to prepare and submit immediately to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment
Report for calendar year 2021, following the prescribed format pursuant to
Items 4.1.A.4g and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW, DBM, DILG and NEDA JMC No.
2013-01.

We further recommended that the GAD AR as prescribed in the JMC be
accompanied by the following: (1) brief summary of the reported program or
project; (2) copies of reported policy issuances; (3) results of HGDG tests; and
(4) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply
Officer to (i) prepare monthly inventory report and stock cards for each item;
and (2) prepare and maintain a Summary/List of donated vaccines received,
distributed and balances.

We recommended that management attach property stickers on all old and
newly acquired property and equipment as a facilitative identification of LGU-
owned property. Moreover, the aforementioned property stickers should be
attached to the newly acquired equipment before these are distributed to the
intended users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC
Chairman/Secretariat to furnish COA with original/certified true copies of the
perfected contracts/purchase orders together with the complete supporting
documents within five (5) working days after approval in compliance with
COA Circular No. 20019-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to stamp PAID all disbursement vouchers and the supporting
documents upon release of payment to prevent the double payment and other
irregular use of the same documents on similar transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to record all materials and supplies
awaiting use in the production process and goods purchased or
produced by an entity which are for distribution to other parties and
recognize it as expense when inventories are sold, exchanged and or
distributed.
b. The supply officer to furnish the copy of acknowledgment receipt or
any proof of receipt of inventory items, by the barangay or the recipient
to the Municipal Accountant for recording the appropriate expense
account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, require the Municipal
Treasurer to verify the reports submitted by the RCC with the Official
Receipts used, the logbook for the released AF to the RCC and conduct the
physical count of the AF in the hands of the collectors at least every turn
over and/or end of the month so as to ensure that all collections are turned
over and completely reported in the RCD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Demand the submission of the remaining liquidation documents from
the Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer and submit the same
to the audit team immediately;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the submission of the
ROD with the related supporting documents to the accounting office
and ensure that the report is supported with the DVs/payrolls with
complete documentation; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the correctness of the
amounts presented in the Report of Disbursements and record only the
cash disbursements or liquidations with disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents duly received by the payee on ensuing
transactions. Further, instruct to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to correct the misstatement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to regularly prepare Report of
Collections and Deposits at the close of each business day, report and remit
all their collections daily, particularly when collection exceeds ₱5,000.00.

We recommended that the LCE require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections and any LGU funds
in her possession intact in the depository bank.
b) The Municipal Accountant to verify the report submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer with the supporting documents and the reported
AF used so as to ensure that all collections and revenues are completely
recorded, and
c) Both the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records to ensure the accuracy and fair presentation of the Financial
Reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
g. Ensure that the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances be strictly imposed.
h. Stop the grant of additional cash advances unless the cash
accountability of the concerned official/employee is fully
settled/liquidated;
i. Refrain from allowing the grant of cash advance for purposes other
than for payment of salaries, wages, and other benefits;
j. Require the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to submit all
the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant and/or refund
any unused/excess cash advance to the Municipal Treasurer
immediately, and submit to the audit team the evidence of liquidation;
k. Require the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to always
observe the timely liquidation of their cash advances in accordance
with Section 5 of COA Circular 97-002. They shall ensure that receipt
of the Report of Disbursements is properly acknowledged by the
Accountant.
l. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the Report of
Disbursements submitted by the AOs, record in the books and submit
the same with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to
the Auditor within ten (10) days after receipt. In case of failure of the
DO to observe timely liquidation of her cash advances, cause the
withholding of her salaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that the cashbooks are handled and maintained
accordingly by adopting the following suggested measures:
a) Require the DO to update the cashbooks regularly, daily or monthly
whichever is practicable, based on the Report of Disbursement.
b) Direct the Disbursing Officer to maintain a separate cashbook for
payroll fund and for operating expenses in each fund.
c) Closely monitor and ensure the accuracy and physical existence of cash
on hand with the cash balance on the cashbook.
d) Reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting record every end
of the month or at least quarterly.
e) Require the DO to immediately take up necessary adjustments on the
books to reflect the correct cash balance and to reconcile with the
accounting records.
f) Require the DO to foot and close the book at the end of each month and
certify its correctness.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Require the MSWDO and LDRRMO and other concerned offices to
provide the accounting unit of the copies of the RIS/Distribution List of
relief goods; and
2. Ensure the submission of the Monthly Report on the Receipt and
Distribution/Utilization/Distribution of Relief Goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection
and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles
subjected to repairs.
2. Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste
Material be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the LCE.
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the
expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle
owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically
repairable.

We recommended that the LCE:
a) Designate/request for plantilla creation for a Supply Officer who shall
be in-charge in the safekeeping and appropriate documentation on the
release of LGU supplies and maintenance of the prescribed registriest
and preparation of reports; and
b) Require the MSWDO, LDRRMO, MHO and other concerned offices
to provide the Supply Officer of the copies of the RIS/Acknowledgment
Receipt and Issuance Form for in-kind donations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
officials responsible in the procurement process to ensure that the LGU only
transact business with the legitimate suppliers/service providers. Likewise,
require the officials concerned to submit the lacking documents mentioned.

We recommended that management strictly observe the guidelines on the
release and utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities
as provided for in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020,
and effect corrections on the Report of Utilization and Status of PAP. We
also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain and provide
copy of a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM account for easy
monitoring/tracking of its utilization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
c) Ensure that the guidelines on the posting and reporting requirements
should be strictly followed in all financial transactions and
program/activity implementation of the LGU; and
d) Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, and
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to observe the
prescribed format in the ensuing utilization reports for the succeeding
funds received from NGAs and other source organizations/agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Continue the initiative in managing the solid wastes of the
municipality and providing support on the programs and projects of the program;
b. Instruct the MENRO and the finance committee to include in the
appropriation of the solid waste management program the
procurement of safety protective equipment and construction of the
toilet/washing facility in the MRF. In addition, coordinate with health
officials on the proper handling and disposal of COVID-19 related
waste materials;
c. Require the HRMO to include in the job descriptions the physical
requirements of the laborers and respiratory fitness of the workers
to be hired;
d. Require the MENRO to determine the safety training courses that
solid waste management workers need and develop a structured
safety training program that includes the criteria on hiring; and
e. Require the MENRO to update the LGU’s 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan, to include provisions for the training needs and
safety of its solid waste workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the posting of
the warranty security by the contractors after the issuance of the Certificate
of Final Acceptance by the agency. To ensure that the warranty security is
posted, the LGU may make it a requirement to observe the one (1) year
period after completion of the project before returning the retention money
after which the warranty period shall commence. Likewise, observe the
period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the imposition of the
warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final acceptance depending
on the nature of the infrastructure project.

We recommend that management refrain from paying regular and recurring expenditures thru the cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer. All payments shall be made thru check except those that are impractical to be paid by check.  We also recommend that the rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances be strictly imposed. Henceforth, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents to validate the adjustments made and to require the Accountable Officers to submit the liquidation documents on all cash advances and/or refund or return any unused funds.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Management still invokes that there were no stores within the municipality that accepts payments by check.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHO to furnish the copy of delivery receipt/Acceptance and Inspection Report of all received medicines and medical supplies to the Accounting Office and the Municipal Accountant to record all inventory items acquired thru purchase and donation in its related inventory account. Likewise, the conduct of inventory taking of supplies be made semestrally as mandated under Sec. 124 of the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The issuance of medicines were monitored and recorded appropriately.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant revise the schedule of all PPE accounts, providing therein the 5% residual value on all items. Likewise, make the necessary adjustments to correct the reported book value of the PPE and Accumulated Depreciation and Prior Year’s Adjustment for the Depreciation Expense recorded during the year.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant adjusted the recorded accumulated depreciation on of PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to render a report on the status/list of the biological assets of the LGU. Likewise, require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of biological assets and furnish copy of Report of Physical Count with complete information on valuation/market value at year-end to the Municipal Accountant to appropriately adjust the recorded valuation and record the plants and trees.
observations.managementAction: The LGU did not conduct physical count of the living animals and render a report thereon, in disregard to PPSAS 27, hence, existence, correctness, and valuation of the recorded amount of ₱1,317,325.00 were not determined.
observations.reason: Absence of personnel to conduct the physical count of the living animals and biological assets and assess its valuation. The Mun. Agriculturist has retired in CY 2019 before they could start the conduct of physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Heads of Office and/or end-user ensure that all the procurement needs of their office in completely and appropriately included in the PPMP; Budget Office evaluate the PPMP and ensure that it include all the planned procurements of the different offices/end users and include only those warranted procurement needs in the proposed budget for approval by the HOPE, and observe the submission of the PPMP to the BAC for consolidation; BAC continuously review its procurement activities and prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in detail; ensure that all procurement activities to be undertaken are included in the plan; The end-user in coordination with the inspection officer, ensure that repairs on motor vehicles and equipment are supported with plans showing the extend of repair to be done, pre- and post-inspection reports; BAC, through its Secretariat, prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies of compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency thereof; and The Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant, ensure that no payment or disbursement should be made for procurement which are not in accordance with the approved budget and APP. Likewise, ensure that all the information needed in the documents attached to the claim, such as but not limited to, Canvass, PR, Notice of Award, IAR and OR, are completely filled-up.
observations.managementAction: Procurement for infra projects and large procuremen t were done thru public bidding
observations.reason: Non-availability of stores within the municipality and no participant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person/committee to attend trainings to enhance their knowledge on gender audit and analysis in order to produce gender responsive GPB, identify gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and include in the GPB the causes of the gender issues, assess the impact of GAD activities and policies in relation to gender equality of the constituents and employees and monitor the GPB implementation.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget was prepared, submitted and approved by the SP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive should: Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office in formulating the implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation aligned with the DSWD-Memorandum Circular No. 02 S of 2014; and Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation, in order to ensure the equality, validity and propriety of such claims. Likewise, ensure the religious preparation and submission of the Utilization Report on DRRM Fund using the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines on the grant of AICS was issued and followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or the Budget Officer to maintain the required registries to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments. Likewise, stop the granting of cash advances to school heads and/or teachers or those who are not duly designated disbursing officers of the LGU; Require the Local School Board (LSB) to formulate the Special Education Fund (SEF) budget taking into consideration the consolidated priority needs from all the school’s heads within the municipality. Likewise, only those PPAs included in the SEF Budget should be funded and disbursed. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete documentation be attached in all the disbursements of the SEF. Likewise, stop the granting of cash advances to school heads and/or teachers or those who are not duly designated disbursing officers of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents for all disbursement was ensured and PPAs under the SEF were implemented by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a biometric machine be provided for the LGUs employees to accurately register the actual office/work hours, require the submission of the DTRs based on the biometric machine entries/records, and require each head of office to monitor the attendance/absences of the personnel under his supervision. In the meantime, the HRMO should review or scrutinize the submitted DTR that it is the actual log-in and log-out of office of an employee based on the logbook to arrive at a correct man hours or attendance. Likewise, the head of office concerned should ensure that the office hours reflected in the DTR is the actual hours an employee is in the office.
observations.managementAction: Purchased the bundy clock and employees were required to log in the logbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor: Prioritize and require the concerned office/officer to monitor the implementation of the programs/projects/activities funded out of the fund transfers from national and/or other agencies; Ensure that the intended programs/projects are implemented in accordance with the trust agreement; Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and submit the required fund utilization report supported with the disbursement vouchers and its related supporting documents within the prescribed period; also, return to the source agency all unused/unexpended funds; Require the Municipal Accountant to effect corrections and/or adjusting entries on the recorded cash advances and disbursements without the duly verified FURs as deduction to the payable accounts/trust liability; and Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation documents of the cash advances totaling ₱1,385,940.00.
observations.managementAction: Appropriat e correcting entries were prepared and substantia l implementa tion of the PPAs from the NGAs funds were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the LGU projects in accordance with the plans and schedules of implementation to establish the cost of unperformed works as at the expiration of the contract time, submit to the audit team the original or certified copies of duly approved Inspection Report and Contractor’s Statement of Work Accomplished as of December 9, 2018, and recommend imposition of Liquidated Damages. The Municipal Accountant to issue Order of Payment for Liquidated Damages as determined and recommended by the Engineer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was instructed to monitor all the infrastructure projects of the LGU and ensure its compliance within the periods stipulated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure all the insurable assets of the LGU be covered with insurance preferably with GSIS in order to be indemnified or compensated from damage to or loss of its properties due to fire, storm or other casualties.
observations.managementAction: Government Vehicles was insured with GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that traveling expenses by LGU Officers and Employees are responsive to the organizational result and development of the Municipality, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial resources of the LGU as required in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-139.
observations.managementAction: Attendance to meetings for private and professional organizations was avoided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adequately disclose the details of all the bank accounts maintained by the LGU and its individual balances and to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the bank statements and endorsed checks.  We likewise recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of stale checks and record the transaction as Accounts Payable and require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the encashment of checks issued and send a written notice to the payee at least one (1) month before a check becomes stale informing him/her the existence of check.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliati on Statements were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the SAAOB and the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and observe the complete submission of financial statements pursuant to Par. 21 of PPSAS 1 and Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: The SAAOB and Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO-designate identify the nature of expenditure of the prior years’ unutilized amount and the Municipal Accountant transfer to the Trust Fund books the unexpended balance of LDRRMF under the Trust Liability-DRRM account as well as maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund by year of transfer and provide appropriate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Likewise, ensure the religious preparation and submission of the Utilization Report on DRRM Fund using the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized LDRRMF was transferre d to the Trust Fund
observations.reason: No reason provided, however, assured the audit team that compliance will be made in CY2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and prepare a 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework as provided for in Sections 12, 16 and 17 of RA 9003 and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval. Moreover, ensure implementation of the same.
observations.managementAction: The SCBAA and Reconcilia tion Statement were prepared and included in the submitted FS as of Dec. 31, 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC, designate and finance committee to submit the Local Development and Financial Plan specifying therein the specific activities and expected results of the programs to ensure that the use of funds will directly benefit the public. Also, require the HRMO to submit the duly certified copy of the JO Contract with details of the expected work to be performed and/or output and require each payee to prepare and submit the accomplishment report duly signed by the immediate supervisor. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the HRMO to prepare the manning schedule with information of the work assignments and functions performed in the attainment of the objectives of the program to prevent duplication of task between them.
observations.managementAction: The following: The MPDC, designate and finance committee to submit the Local Development and Financial Plan specifying therein the specific activities and expected results of the programs to ensure that the use of funds will directly benefit the public. Also, require the HRMO to submit the duly certified copy of the JO Contract with details of the expected work to be performed and/or output and require each payee to prepare and submit the accomplishment report duly signed by the immediate supervisor.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to prepare the plan and manning schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006, to avoid any administrative disciplinary action.
observations.managementAction: The Check disburseme nt and Cash Collection transacto n documents were submitted regularly.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of the cash disbursement documents from the Disbursing Officer due to the volume of the cash advances granted and for liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


         ➢  Require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  verify  the  Report   of
           Collections and Deposits  (RCD) submitted by the RCC  and  conduct
           the physical count of the AF in the hands of  the  collectors  at
           least every end of the month  to  report  completely  the  actual
           collection at the end of each period.


         ➢ Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the RCD  submitted  by
           the Municipal Treasurer with the  supporting  documents  and  the
           reported AF used  so  as  to  ensure  that  all  collections  and
           revenues are completely recorded.


         ➢  Moreover,  require  both  the  Accountant  and   the   Municipal
           Treasurer to reconcile their records to ensure the  accuracy  and
           fair presentation of the Financial Reports.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the
        Municipal Treasurer to direct all collecting officers  to  regularly
        prepare Report of Collections and Deposits  at  the  close  of  each
        business  day,  report  and  remit  all  their  collections   daily,
        particularly when collection exceeds ₱5,000.00

We recommended that the  LCE  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
        deposit intact the collections and any LGU funds in her possession.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


   a. Demand the submission of  liquidation  documents  from  the  Municipal
      Treasurer and Disbursing Officer and submit the same to the audit team
      immediately;


   b. Ensure that the Municipal Treasurer and  Disbursing  Officer  strictly
      observe the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash  advances
      as prescribed by COA Circular No. 97-002; and


   c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify  the  correctness  of  the
      amounts presented in the Report of Disbursements and record  only  the
      cash disbursements or  liquidations  with  disbursement  vouchers  and
      supporting  documents  duly  received  by   the   payee   on   ensuing
      transactions.


        This audit recommendation is without prejudice to  the  issuance  of
        audit suspension and disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


        a. Ensure that the rules and regulations on the grant,  utilization
           and liquidation of cash advances be strictly imposed.


        b. Stop the grant of  additional  cash  advances  unless  the  cash
           accountability  of  the  concerned  official/employee  is  fully
           settled/liquidated;


        c. Refrain from allowing the grant of  cash  advance  for  purposes
           other than for payment of salaries, wages, and other benefits;


        d. Require the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to submit
           all the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant and/or
           refund any unused/excess cash advance to the Municipal Treasurer
           immediately, and submit  to  the  audit  team  the  evidence  of
           liquidation;


        e. Require the Disbursing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to always
           observe  the  timely  liquidation  of  their  cash  advances  in
           accordance with Section 5 of COA Circular  97-002.   They  shall
           ensure that receipt of the Report of Disbursements  is  properly
           acknowledged by the Accountant.


        f. Instruct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  verify  the  Report  of
           Disbursements submitted by the AOs,  record  in  the  books  and
           submit the same with all the  vouchers/payrolls  and  supporting
           documents to the Auditor within ten  (10)  days  after  receipt.
           In case of failure of the DO to observe  timely  liquidation  of
           her cash advances, cause the withholding of her salaries.


        This audit recommendation is without prejudice to  the  issuance  of
        Notice of Disallowance or Suspension.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:


        a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of all the
           municipal road networks and render a report thereon; and


        b. Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO  and
           Municipal  Engineer  to  reconcile  the  Road   Network   amount
           presented in the financial  statement  and  the  report  of  the
           Municipal Engineer; and


        c. Instruct the Accountant to strictly follow the provision of  COA
           Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for
           local road and road network system to ensure that all roads  are
           properly  valued  and  recorded,  and   to   assure   the   fair
           presentation  of  the  account  infrastructure  assets  in   the
           financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  Inventory
        Committee to include the work/zoo animals and breeding stocks in the
        conduct of the physical count and render a report  thereon  so  that
        the   Municipal   Accountant   can   appropriately    provide    the
        depreciation/impairment on these  assets  and  adjust  the  recorded
        valuation.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
        Municipal  Engineer   to   monitor   the   implementation   of   the
        infrastructure projects in  the  barangays  to  which  the  LGU  has
        extended assistance.  Likewise, ensure that all transfers  (in  kind
        or in cash) shall be supported with the Program of Works  so  as  to
        determine  the  regularity  of  the  materials  or  amount  granted.
        Further, a Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement should be entered
        into by and between the recipient agency and the  LGU  which  should
        include provision that the materials shall be used for its  intended
        purpose, the  period  of  implementation  of  the  project  and  the
        submission of the accomplishment report or proof  of  the  completed
        project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


        a. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program  of  Works,
           Status  of  Work  Accomplished,  Agency  Inspection  Report  and
           Certificate of Completion and Acceptance for the  infrastructure
           projects   implemented    by    administration    totaling    to
           ₱2,295,318.23, to establish the validity and regularity  of  the
           purchases of construction materials.


        b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments  of
           labor should be supported with the Status of  Work  Accomplished
           and/or Accomplishment Report which should be compared  with  the
           expected days to complete the infrastructure project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


        a. Require the  HRMO  to  submit  a  record  of  the  JO  employees
           detailing the expected work to be performed  and/or  output  and
           require each payee to  prepare  and  submit  the  accomplishment
           report duly signed by the immediate supervisor.


        b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the rate per contract
           as basis for the computation of payment of wages  to  job  order
           employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


        a.  Direct  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  (BAC)   to   procure
           consultancy services pursuant to the provisions in Republic  Act
           No. 9184.


        b. Require the Human Resource Management  Officer  to  observe  the
           rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants and to assess
           the necessity of the services of consultant in  order  to  avoid
           unnecessary  expenditure  of  government  funds,  and   evaluate
           whether  the  responsibilities  of   the   existing   consultant
           duplicate those of the regular employees.


        c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents
           to support the payments to the consultant.


        d. Direct the Municipal Accountant that henceforth he should ensure
           that  payments  for  consultancy  services  are  supported  with
           complete documentation.


           This audit recommendation is without prejudice  to  issuance  of
           Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the  BAC  to  ensure
        that  the  contracts  should  include  all  the  necessary  details,
        particularly, the delivery schedule,  for  the  enforcement  of  the
        contract and possible claims against the supplier.   Also,  require
        the BAC to validate the eligibility and capability of  the  supplier
        to  deliver  the  contracted  items  during  the  post-qualification
        proceeding to avoid the awarding of contracts to ineligible bidders.

We recommended that the LCE instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
        ensure that all transaction of the LGU are supported  with  complete
        documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.    Submit  the
        lacking documents required and register the  Deed  of  Sale  on  the
        subject lots with the Register of Deeds in order to  facilitate  the
        issuance of a certificate of title in favor of the municipality.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  install  control  and
        monitoring mechanisms to ensure that the use of government  vehicles
        and consumption of  fuel  shall  strictly  be  for  a  valid  public
        purpose.   Henceforth,  the  following  documents  and  reports   be
        required on the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption:


        a) Duly accomplished and serially numbered  Driver’s  Trip  Tickets
           approved by the Municipal Mayor or his authorized representative
           to control the use of government vehicle;


        b) Monthly Report  of  Official  Travels  prepared  by  the  driver
           concerned and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report  prepared  by
           the equipment custodian summarizing in chronological  order  his
           trips or use of equipment, respectively,  for  the  month,  duly
           certified by the  driver  and  approved  by  the  Administrative
           Officer.


        c) Report of Fuel  Consumption  shall  be  prepared  and  submitted
           monthly to the auditor for verification  purposes.   The  report
           shall show the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the
           agency during the month, total  distance  traveled,  total  fuel
           consumed and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:


        a) Municipal  Treasurer  to  conduct  intensive  tax  campaign  and
           information drive in order to encourage more  taxpayers  to  pay
           taxes;


        b) Impose the legal remedies provided for, and exercise its  taxing
           authority mandated under the Local Government Code of 1991; and


        c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer  to  reconcile  the
           RPT and SET receivable balances.

We recommended to the Local Chief  Executive  to  give  preferential
        attention on the  information  dissemination  programs  relating  to
        disaster preparedness to enable the community to effectively respond
        to disastrous events and conditions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


   a. Require the MENRO to determine the safety training courses that  solid
      waste management workers need and develop a structured safety training
      program that includes the criteria on hiring; and


   b. Provide toilets and hand washing facilities in the Materials  Recovery
      Facility.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

        a. Require the  Municipal  Accountant  or  the  Budget  Officer  to
           maintain the required registries to prevent  the  incurrence  of
           expenditures in excess of allotments.


        b. Coordinate with the SB Committee on Education and the  concerned
           personnel to formulate policies and guidelines in the  grant  of
           the scholarship allowance and  ensure  that  the  policy  should
           include  provisions  for  the  criteria  in  the  selection  and
           retention of eligible scholars.


        c. Designate personnel to monitor the implementation  of  student’s
           financial assistance to ensure the effectiveness of the program.


        d.   Require   the    scholars    to    submit    Certificate    of
           Registration/Enrollment Form,  and  Report  of  Rating  for  the
           financial assistance,  in  order  to  ensure  the  validity  and
           propriety of such claims. Moreover, a standard  rate  should  be
           required for admission as a scholar and a grade to be maintained
           for the continuing scholars.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  demand  the  immediate
        delivery of the remaining 14 units from the supplier and  issue  the
        same to  the  recipients.   Otherwise,  enforce  the  sanctions  and
        penalties to  the  defaulting  contractor,  such  as  imposition  of
        administrative suspension for one to two years from participating in
        all the government bidding process,  termination  of  the  contract,
        forfeiture of  the  performance  security  and  enforcement  of  the
        liquidated damages, as provided for in Republic Act 9184.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
        Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and  submit  the  required  fund
        utilization report supported with the disbursement vouchers and  its
        related supporting documents within the  prescribed  period,  also,
        return to the source agency all unused/unexpended funds.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to avoid  the  conduct  of
        out-of-town meetings/conferences/seminars which can be  held  within
        the  municipal  premises,  bearing  in  mind  the  prudent  use   of
        government funds without sacrificing effectiveness.

        This audit recommendation is without prejudice of  the  issuance  of
        Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  Municipal
        Treasurer and Municipal Accountant  to  immediately  submit  to  the
        audit team the accounts subjected in the Notices of Suspension.   We
        further recommend to the  LCE  to  ensure  the  enforcement  of  the
        settlement of all suspended transactions.

We recommended that management strictly comply with  the  provisions
        of Revenue Regulation No. 1093 dated June 6, 1993 and Section 272 of
        RA No. 8424 to generate more revenues for the government  and  avoid
        the penalties that maybe  instituted  for  non-compliance  with  the
        aforecited regulations.

e) BAC, through its Secretariat, prepare the Procurement Monitoring
Report (PMR) and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every
semester, as required, to allow monitoring by appropriate government
Oversight Bodies of compliance with the provisions of the
Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency thereof; and
f) The Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant, ensure that no
payment or disbursement should be made for procurement which are
not in accordance with the approved budget and APP. Likewise,
ensure that all the information needed in the documents attached to
the claim, such as but not limited to, Canvass, PR, Notice of Award,
IAR and OR, are completely filled-up.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal
Person/committee to attend trainings to enhance their knowledge on
gender audit and analysis in order to produce gender responsive GPB,
identify gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and include in the
GPB the causes of the gender issues, assess the impact of GAD activities
and policies in relation to gender equality of the constituents and
employees and monitor the GPB implementation.

8.8 We recommended that Local Chief Executive should:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office
in formulating the implementing guidelines to regulate the financial
aid or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation
aligned with the DSWD-Memorandum Circular No. 02 s of 2014; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to
require from the applicants for AICS complete supporting documents
for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and
recommendation, in order to ensure the equality, validity and
propriety of such claims.

9.9 We recommended the following:
a. Require the school heads and/or teachers to submit the liquidation
report supported with complete documentation on the cash advance
granted amounting to ₱136,000.00;
b. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that complete
documentation be attached in all the disbursements of the SEF.
Likewise, stop the granting of cash advances to school heads and/or
teachers or those who are not duly designated disbursing officers of
the LGU; and
c. Require the Local School Board (LSB) to formulate the Special
Education Fund (SEF) budget taking into consideration the
consolidated priority needs from all the school heads within the
municipality. Likewise, only those PPAs included in the SEF Budget
should be funded and disbursed.

10.7 We recommended that a biometric machine be provided for the LGUs
employees to accurately register the actual office/work hours, require the
submission of the DTRs based on the biometric machine entries/records,
and require each head of office to monitor the attendance/absences of the
personnel under his supervision. In the meantime, the HRMO should
review or scrutinize the submitted DTR that it is the actual log-in and log-
out of office of an employee based on the logbook to arrive at a correct
man hours or attendance. Likewise, the head of office concerned should
ensure that the office hours reflected in the DTR is the actual hours an
employee is in the office.

11.9 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to
the Municipal Mayor:
a. Prioritize and require the concerned office/officer to monitor the
implementation of the programs/projects/activities funded out of the
fund transfers from national and/or other agencies;
b. Ensure that the intended programs/projects are implemented in
accordance with the trust agreement;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and
submit the required fund utilization report supported with the
disbursement vouchers and its related supporting documents within
the prescribed period, also, return to the source agency all
unused/unexpended funds;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect corrections and/or
adjusting entries on the recorded cash advances and disbursements
without the duly verified FURs as deduction to the payable
accounts/trust liability; and
e. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation documents
of the cash advances totaling ₱1,385,940.00.

12.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) The Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the
LGU projects in accordance with the plans and schedules of
implementation to establish the cost of performed works as at the
expiration of the contract time, submit to the audit team the original or
certified copies of duly approved Inspection Report and Contractor’s
Statement of Work Accomplished as of December 9, 2018, and
recommend imposition of Liquidated Damages.
b) The Municipal Accountant to issue Order of Payment for Liquidated
Damages as determined and recommended by the Engineer.

13.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure all the insurable
assets of the LGU be covered with insurance with GSIS in order to be
indemnified or compensated from damage to or loss of its properties due
to fire, storm or other casualties.

14.7 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that
traveling expenses by LGU Officers and Employees are responsive to the
organizational result and development outcome of the Municipality,
keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial resources of the
LGU as required in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-139.
